full_narration: Take a look at this. Beautiful, rare, and absolutely priceless. ... Or at least, that is what you have been told your entire life. For over a century, you, your parents, and your grandparents have been the silent victims of the single greatest marketing heist in human history. A highly calculated campaign designed to manufacture desire out of thin air. In reality, diamonds are not rare, they are not an investment, and they are certainly not priceless. ... They are common carbon stones, controlled by a single family who convinced the world that a shiny rock was the ultimate measure of your love. It began in the late 1930s.  Inside a quiet, wood-paneled office in London, the executives of the De Beers diamond cartel were facing a terrifying reality.  ...
